revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''相輪橖'''（そうりんとう）は、[[仏教]]施設のひとつである。 [[仏塔]]の一種と考えられ、[[三重塔]]や[[五重塔]]の[[屋...'
revision.text:
'''相輪橖'''（そうりんとう）は、[[仏教]]施設のひとつである。
[[仏塔]]の一種と考えられ、[[三重塔]]や[[五重塔]]の[[屋根]]の部分を取り払い、[[柱]]と仏塔の屋根の上にある[[相輪]]の部分からなり、四方に柱を支える支柱がある。
古くから建てられていたようであるが、現在歴史のある相輪橖は少なく、[[比叡山延暦寺]]や[[日光]][[輪王寺]]にある。
==関連項目== *[[塔]] *[[仏塔]] *[[三重塔]] *[[五重塔]]
[[category:仏教建築|そうりんとう]] {{stub}}

'''相輪橖'''（そうりんとう）は、[[仏教]]施設のひとつである。
[[仏塔]]の一種と考えられ、[[三重塔]]や[[五重塔]]の[[屋根]]の部分を取り払い、[[柱]]と仏塔の屋根の上にある[[相輪]]の部分からなり、四方に柱を支える支柱がある。
古くから建てられていたようであるが、現在歴史のある相輪橖は少なく、[[比叡山延暦寺]]や[[日光]][[輪王寺]]にある。
==関連項目== *[[塔]] *[[仏塔]] *[[三重塔]] *[[五重塔]]
[[category:寺院建築|そうりんとう]] {{stub}}

'''相輪橖'''（そうりんとう）は、[[仏教]]施設のひとつである。'''相輪塔'''とも書く。
[[仏塔]]の一種と考えられ、[[三重塔]]や[[五重塔]]の[[屋根]]の部分を取り払い、[[柱]]と仏塔の屋根の上にある[[相輪]]の部分からなり、四方に柱を支える支柱がある。
古くから建てられていたようであるが、現在歴史のある相輪橖は少なく、[[比叡山延暦寺]]や[[日光]][[輪王寺]]にある。
==関連項目== *[[塔]] *[[仏塔]] *[[三重塔]] *[[五重塔]]
[[category:寺院建築|そうりんとう]] {{stub}}

'''相輪橖'''（そうりんとう）は、[[仏教]]施設のひとつである。'''相輪塔'''とも書く。
[[仏塔]]の一種と考えられ、[[三重塔]]や[[五重塔]]の[[屋根]]の部分を取り払い、[[柱]]と仏塔の屋根の上にある[[相輪]]の部分からなり、四方に柱を支える支柱がある。
古くから建てられていたようであるが、現在歴史のある相輪橖は少なく、[[比叡山延暦寺]]や[[日光]][[輪王寺]]にある。
==関連項目== *[[塔]] *[[仏塔]] *[[三重塔]] *[[五重塔]]
[[category:寺院建築]] {{DEFAULTSORT:そうりんとう}} {{buddhism stub}}

'''相輪橖'''（そうりんとう）は、[[仏教]]施設のひとつである。'''相輪塔'''とも書く。
ロケットのような形をしている。
高さは13.7ｍある。
[[仏塔]]の一種と考えられ、[[三重塔]]や[[五重塔]]の[[屋根]]の部分を取り払い、[[柱]]と仏塔の屋根の上にある[[相輪]]の部分からなり、四方に柱を支える支柱がある。
古くから建てられていたようであるが、現在歴史のある相輪橖は少なく、[[比叡山延暦寺]]や[[日光山|日光]][[輪王寺]]にある。

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sōrin-tō]][[ja:相輪橖]][[nl:Sōrintō]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11582430]] Wikidata name: sōrintō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Dutch derived stuff[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 7 April 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Source Citation|date=June 2023}} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|nl|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|ja|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|zh|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|lt=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_ja=零羊崎神社|lt_nl=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_zh=零羊崎神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|qq=|1=HItsujisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660144}} Sōrintō]]
'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|qq=draft|lt=Five-storied pagoda|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tairyūji|Tairyu-ji Temple]] </gallery>
==List== {| class="wikitable" |+ !Name !City/Municipality !Year Built (Saichō's Initiation) !Year Built (Existing) ! !Designated Cultural Property !Notes |- |Enryaku-ji East Tower |Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |820 | |No longer existing | |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Enryaku-ji West Tower |Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |820 |1892 |Existing |National Important Cultural Property |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Former Ryokunji (Jōhō-ji) |Fujioka, Gunma Prefecture |815 |1672 |Existing |Fujioka City-designated Tangible Cultural Property |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Daiji Temple |Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture |815 |1725 |Existing |Tochigi Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Former Kamadozan-ji (Dazaifu Tenmangū) |Dazaifu, Fukuoka Prefecture |814 |1847 |Existing |Dazaifu City-designated Tangible Cultural Property |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Former Usami Miroku-ji (Usa Shrine) |Usa, Oita Prefecture |814 | |No longer existing | |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Sairen-ji |Namegata, Ibaraki Prefecture | |1287 |Existing |National Important Cultural Property | |- |Rinno-ji |Nikko, Tochigi Prefecture | |1647 |Existing |National Important Cultural Property | |- |Former Chōzen-ji (Reiyousaki Shrine) |Ishinomaki, Miyagi Prefecture | |1810 |Existing |Ishinomaki City-designated Tangible Cultural Property | |- |Tairyu-ji Temple |Anan, Tokushima Prefecture | |1816 |Existing |Not designated | |}
==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>
{{Navigation pagodas in Japan}}

(since 2001) [https://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/an/Religion-in-Japan ''Religion in Japan: A Web Handbook''] University of Vienna, Austrian Academy of Sciences, Institute for the Cultural and Intellectual History of Asia (accessed: 29-2-2020) }}

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').

Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

[[ja:相輪橖]] [[nl:Sōrintō]] [[de:Sourintō]] [[nl:Sorintou]] [[Category:Temple Architecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sōrin-tō}} {{wikidata link|Q11582430}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sōrin-tō]][[ja:相輪橖]][[nl:Sōrintō]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582430]] Wikidata name: sōrintō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Dutch derived stuff[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 7 April 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Source Citation|date=June 2023}} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|nl|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|ja|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|zh|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|lt=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_ja=零羊崎神社|lt_nl=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_zh=零羊崎神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|qq=|1=HItsujisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660144}} Sōrintō]]
'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|qq=draft|lt=Five-storied pagoda|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tairyūji|Tairyu-ji Temple]] </gallery>

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').

{{Source Citation|date=June 2023}} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|nl|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|ja|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|zh|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|lt=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_ja=零羊崎神社|lt_nl=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_zh=零羊崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|1=HItsujisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660144}} Sōrintō]]
'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tairyūji|Tairyu-ji Temple]] </gallery>

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sōrin-tō]][[ja:相輪橖]][[nl:Sōrintō]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582430]] Wikidata name: sōrintō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Dutch derived stuff[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 7 April 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Source Citation|date=June 2023}} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|nl|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|ja|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|zh|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|lt=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_ja=零羊崎神社|lt_nl=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_zh=零羊崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|1=HItsujisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660144}} Sōrintō]]
'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tairyūji|Tairyu-ji Temple]] </gallery>

Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').

Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

[[ja:相輪橖]] [[nl:Sōrintō]] [[de:Sourintō]] [[nl:Sorintou]] [[Category:Temple Architecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sōrin-tō}} {{wikidata link|Q11582430}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sōrin-tō]][[ja:相輪橖]][[nl:Sōrintō]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582430]] Wikidata name: sōrintō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Dutch derived stuff[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 7 April 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Source Citation|date=June 2023}} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|nl|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|ja|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|zh|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|lt=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_ja=零羊崎神社|lt_nl=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_zh=零羊崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|1=HItsujisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660144}} Sōrintō]]
'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tairyūji|Tairyu-ji Temple]] </gallery>

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tairyūji|Tairyu-ji Temple]] </gallery>

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sōrin-tō]][[ja:相輪橖]][[nl:Sōrintō]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582430]] Wikidata name: sōrintō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Dutch derived stuff[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 7 April 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Source Citation|date=June 2023}} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|nl|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|ja|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|zh|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|lt=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_ja=零羊崎神社|lt_nl=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_zh=零羊崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|1=HItsujisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660144}} Sōrintō]]
'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tairyūji|Tairyu-ji Temple]] </gallery>

The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').

Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

[[ja:相輪橖]] [[nl:Sōrintō]] [[de:Sourintō]] [[nl:Sorintou]] [[Category:Temple Architecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sōrin-tō}} {{wikidata link|Q11582430}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sōrin-tō]][[ja:相輪橖]][[nl:Sōrintō]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sōrin-tō]][[ja:相輪橖]][[nl:Sōrintō]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582430]] Wikidata name: sōrintō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Dutch derived stuff[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:44, 7 April 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Source Citation|date=June 2023}} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|nl|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|ja|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|zh|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|lt=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_ja=零羊崎神社|lt_nl=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_zh=零羊崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|1=HItsujisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660144}} Sōrintō]]
'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tairyūji|Tairyu-ji Temple]] </gallery>
==List== {| class="wikitable" |+ !Name !City/Municipality !Year Built (Saichō's Initiation) !Year Built (Existing) ! !Designated Cultural Property !Notes |- |Enryaku-ji East Tower |Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |820 | |No longer existing | |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Enryaku-ji West Tower |Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |820 |1892 |Existing |National Important Cultural Property |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Former Ryokunji (Jōhō-ji) |Fujioka, Gunma Prefecture |815 |1672 |Existing |Fujioka City-designated Tangible Cultural Property |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Daiji Temple |Tochigi City, Tochigi Prefecture |815 |1725 |Existing |Tochigi Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Former Kamadozan-ji (Dazaifu Tenmangū) |Dazaifu, Fukuoka Prefecture |814 |1847 |Existing |Dazaifu City-designated Tangible Cultural Property |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Former Usami Miroku-ji (Usa Shrine) |Usa, Oita Prefecture |814 | |No longer existing | |Rokushō Pagoda |- |Sairen-ji |Namegata, Ibaraki Prefecture | |1287 |Existing |National Important Cultural Property | |- |Rinno-ji |Nikko, Tochigi Prefecture | |1647 |Existing |National Important Cultural Property | |- |Former Chōzen-ji (Reiyousaki Shrine) |Ishinomaki, Miyagi Prefecture | |1810 |Existing |Ishinomaki City-designated Tangible Cultural Property | |- |Tairyu-ji Temple |Anan, Tokushima Prefecture | |1816 |Existing |Not designated | |}
==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}

It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').

{{Source Citation|date=June 2023}} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|nl|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|ja|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|zh|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|lt=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_ja=零羊崎神社|lt_nl=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_zh=零羊崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|1=HItsujisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660144}} Sōrintō]]
'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tairyūji|Tairyu-ji Temple]] </gallery>

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.

A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').

Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

{{Source Citation|date=June 2023}} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|nl|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|ja|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|zh|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|lt=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_ja=零羊崎神社|lt_nl=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_zh=零羊崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|1=HItsujisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660144}} Sōrintō]]
'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 [[:en:Metre|m]]}}. It is considered a type of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]], and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the [[:en:Sōrin|ring of eaves]] located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, [[:en:Rinnō-ji|Nikkoyama Rinno-ji]], and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Tairyūji|Tairyu-ji Temple]] </gallery>

==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 {{ill|Metre|en|Metre|WD=Q11573|lt=m}}}}. It is considered a type of {{ill|Stupa|en|Stupa|WD=Q180987|lt=Buddhist Pagoda}}, and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the {{ill|Sōrin|en|Sōrin|WD=Q834370|lt=ring of eaves}} located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, {{ill|Rinnō-ji|en|Rinnō-ji|WD=Q1348249|lt=Nikkoyama Rinno-ji}}, and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.

==Dutch Overview==
{|class="vatop" style="border:solid thin slategray;background-color:aliceblue;font-size:90%;" align="right" !colspan=6 style="font-size:120%;"| The components of the ''sōrin'' from top to bottom: |- | ||  || || ||rowspan=10| [[File:Tenneiji_Onomichi03s3200-Sourin.jpg|300px|center]] |- | ''hōju'' || || 宝珠 || 'sacred jewel' |- | ''ryūsha'' || || 竜車 || 'dragon wheel' |- | ''suien'' || || 水煙 || 'water flame' |- | ''sakkan'' || || || column, with: |- |… ''fūtaku'' || || 風鐸, ふうたく  || wind chimes |- |… ''kurin'' || || 九輪 || nine rings |- | ''ukebana'' || || 受花 || {{nowrap|'receptive flower'}} |- | ''fukubachi''|| || 伏鉢 || inverted bowl |- | ''roban'' || || 露盤 || 'dew basin' |}
'''sōrintō'''<ref group="note">''Sōrintō'' - {{Citation foreign Wikipedia|lang=ja|title=相輪橖|oldid=78213116|date=20200628}}</ref> (相輪橖, 相輪塔, そうりんとう) is a type of small {{ill|pagoda|Pagode|Pagode|塔|塔|塔|塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pagoda|qq=}} that consists solely of a pillar (''sao'') and a sōrin. The sōrintō is also known as the Aiwa‑ or Siwa‑pagoda.
A ''sōrin'' (相輪)<ref>{{aut|JAANUS}} (2010) [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/s/sourin.htm ''Sourin'' 相輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref> is the most important part of a pagoda. It is also the spire or finial of a pagoda—the vertical axis that is generally used as a decorative element rising from the top of the structure. The ''sōrin'' is made of metal (bronze or iron) and is placed atop a wooden pagoda or carved into a stone pagoda (''sekitō''). It usually consists of several components: at the base is the box‑shaped “dew basin” (''roban''), surmounted by the inverted bowl (''fukubachi''); above that is a pillar (''sakkan'') with nine rings (''kurin'', 空輪 or ''hōrin'' (宝輪)) which may be adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''), then the “water flame” (''suien'') follows; and finally, crowning the structure, the “dragon wheel” (''ryūsha'', not found on stone pagodas, ''sekitō'') is topped with the “sacred jewel” (''hōju'').
''Kūrin''<ref>JAANUS, [http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kurin.htm ''Kurin'' 九輪], Japanese Architectural and Art Net Users System</ref>, or ''hōrin'' (宝輪) is a component of the top of a pagoda, the ''sōrin''. Typically, nine metal rings are affixed to the tubular metal casing that covers the tapering central pillar rising above the pagoda’s roof. These rings may be decorated with wind chimes (''fūtaku'').
<gallery widths="200px" heights="260px" perrow="4" class="center" caption="Images"> Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|A ''sorintō'' at Hitsujisaki {{ill|shrine|Schrein|Schrein|神社|神社|神社|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} 20190121 Tōdai-ji sōrin-1.jpg|A ''sorintō'' at Tōdai-ji Sorinto.jpg|''Sōrintō'', where the rings are adorned with wind chimes (''fūtaku''). Sorinto LACMA M.91.377.71.jpg|LACMA (1865) Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Sairen-ji Temple in {{ill|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|Namegata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namegata|qq=}} Sorinto Tower in Dazaifu Temman Shrine.jpg|''Sōrintō'' at Dazaifu Temman Shrine Murouji gojyunoto sorin.jpg|Murōji ''gojū-no-tō'' with a ''sōrin'' atop the roof </gallery>

{{Source Citation|date=June 2023}} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja Sourintou.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|nl|Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|ja|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|zh|零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|lt=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_ja=零羊崎神社|lt_nl=Reiyousaki Shrine|lt_zh=零羊崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiyousaki Shrine (Ishinomaki City Minato)|1=HItsujisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660144}} Sōrintō]]
'''Sorintou''' (相輪橖) is one of the {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} facilities. It is also written as '''Sorintou'''.
==Overview== It has a shape resembling a rocket, and its height is {{Nowrap|13.7 {{ill|Metre|en|Metre|WD=Q11573|lt=m}}}}. It is considered a type of {{ill|Stupa|en|Stupa|WD=Q180987|lt=Buddhist Pagoda}}, and by removing the roof sections of {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|zh|三重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} and {{ill|Five-storied pagoda|8=ja|9=五重塔|lt=Five-storied pagoda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied pagoda|qq=}}, it is composed of pillars and the {{ill|Sōrin|en|Sōrin|WD=Q834370|lt=ring of eaves}} located on top of the pagoda roof, with supporting pillars extending in four directions. Although they appear to have been constructed long ago, few historical Sorintou remain today, with examples found at {{ill|Enryaku-ji Temple|nl|Enryaku-ji Temple|ja|比叡山延暦寺|zh|比叡山延暦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku-ji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦寺|1=Enryaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917195}}, {{ill|Rinnō-ji|en|Rinnō-ji|WD=Q1348249|lt=Nikkoyama Rinno-ji}}, and at Nishiren-ji in Namegata City.
<gallery> Sorinto.jpg|Enryaku-ji West Tower Sorinto of Rinno Temple.jpg|Rinno-ji Temple Sorinto of Sairen-ji Temple in Namegata city,IBARAKI,Japan.jpg|Ibaraki · {{ill|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|nl|Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|ja|西蓮寺 (行方市)|zh|西莲寺 (行方市)|lt=Nishiren-ji|lt_ja=西蓮寺|lt_nl=Nishiren-ji|lt_zh=西莲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiren-ji Temple (Namegata)|qq=}} Tairyu-ji, Sōrin Tower 01.jpg|{{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Tairyūji|en|Tairyūji|WD=Q11444739|lt=Tairyu-ji Temple}} </gallery>
